title: EU Commission to present series of measures at EUCO Cyprus meeting
summary:
Robust intra-EU coordination, measures member states might apply to better protect vulnerable households and sectors from high energy prices, and ways to reduce energy demand are among the measures that the European Commission will present at the European Council meeting in Cyprus soon. 
The protection measures should be targeted to vulnerable groups, timely and temporary, Commission president said.

title: UK export orders stay weak despite slight Q1 uptick: BCC survey
summary:
UK exporters, especially SMEs, continue to struggle, with only 25 per cent reporting higher orders in Q1, according to BCC. 
Smaller firms and services exporters lag behind larger players. 
While slightly improved from late 2025, export performance remains below pre-2018 levels. 
BCC warned of rising headwinds and urged stronger government support and trade facilitation to boost overseas growth.

title: Australian business confidence plunges in March amid uncertainty: NAB
summary:
Australian business confidence plunged in March, falling 29 points to -29, while business conditions remained relatively stable, according to NAB. 
Despite strong capacity utilisation, forward orders and capital expenditure weakened, signalling rising uncertainty. 
Cost pressures intensified, with purchase costs doubling. 
While some regions saw improved conditions, confidence declined nationwide.

title: Techtextil & Texprocess 2026 honour 17 global innovations
summary:
Techtextil and Texprocess Innovation Awards 2026 recognise 17 global winners across ten categories for breakthrough textile innovations.
From PFAS-free finishes and AI-driven production to circular materials and smart applications, the awards highlight solutions shaping sustainability, automation and cross-industry transformation in textiles and beyond.

title: South India cotton yarn steady amid weak demand, uncertainty
summary:
South India cotton yarn prices remained steady amid weak demand and thin trade in Mumbai and Tiruppur due to holiday and market uncertainty. 
Buyers stayed cautious following the ceasefire and global tensions, limiting purchases. 
Stable cotton prices and cautious mill buying further reflected subdued sentiment, though seasonal demand offered some support.

title: The bill from the war is here—Small brands will pay first
summary:
Rising war-led costs are beginning to flow through supply chains, with large brands initially absorbing shocks but now passing them on. 
Smaller brands, constrained by thin margins and limited sourcing flexibility, face acute pressure. 
With input costs up 20 to 30 per cent, many are forced to pivot materials, scale down production, or absorb losses, threatening their viability.

title: Indian ministry signs MoUs with Porter, Gigin to boost employment
summary:
The Indian Ministry of Labour & Employment yesterday signed MoUs with SmartShift Logistics Solutions Pvt. Ltd. (Porter) and Gigin Technologies to expand employment opportunities and strengthen digital job matching through the National Career Service (NCS) portal. 
Porter aims at generating nearly 600,000 opportunities annually through the NCS platform and around 3 million opportunities by 2030.

title: Normalisation of commodity supplies to take time: IMF, World Bank, IEA
summary:
While the situation triggered by the Iran war stays uncertain and shipping through the Strait of Hormuz is yet to normalise, even after regular shipping resumes, it will take time for global supplies of key commodities to normalise, the heads of the IEA, the IMF and the World Bank Group said. 
Fuel and fertiliser prices may remain high for a prolonged period given the damage to infrastructure, they said.

title: Germany unveils $1.9-bn fuel price relief package amid energy shock
summary:
Germany yesterday announced a €1.6-billion ($1.9-billion) fuel price relief package for households and businesses struggling with the energy shock triggered by the Middle East conflict. 
Chancellor Friedrich Merz said his government has decided to cut the tax on petrol and diesel by around $0.19 for two months. 
The funds for the relief measures would be financed by higher taxes on tobacco.

title: ASEAN+3 nations must safeguard fiscal viability, rebuild buffers: AMRO
summary:
With fiscal positions weakened and policy space narrowed, ASEAN+3 policymakers must safeguard fiscal sustainability and rebuild buffers, the ASEAN+3 Fiscal Policy Report 2026 said. 
Governments should also support growth, facilitate structural transformation and reduce poverty and inequality over the medium to long term, it noted. 
Particular attention should be given to liabilities outside the budget.

title: UK’s Sosandar returns to profitability amid robust FY26 performance
summary:
Sosandar reported FY26 revenue of £42.3 million (~$57.53 million), up 14 per cent, driven by strong online growth, with own-site sales rising 24 per cent. 
The company returned to profitability with PBT of £0.4 million (~$0.54 million) and improved margins. 
Despite slightly missing revenue expectations, performance remained solid. 
Strong third-party sales supported confidence in profitable growth.

title: Germany’s Puma appoints James Carnes to new creative leadership role
summary:
Puma has appointed James Carnes as senior vice president creative direction.
Reporting to Maria Valdes, he will oversee creative direction, innovation, and product excellence. 
With over two decades of experience, including leadership roles at Adidas, he will align design strategy with business goals to strengthen Puma’s global brand appeal and market position.

title: Ireland’s Primark boosts circular fashion with updated strategy
summary:
Primark has launched Circular Product Standard 2.0 to expand circular fashion across its range. 
The framework focuses on durability, sustainable materials and recyclability, covering nine product categories. 
In FY 24/25, 5 per cent of products were circular by design, including 20 per cent of jersey. 
A new ‘Progressive’ level raises standards, backed by training and practical design improvements.

title: Hong Kong apparel imports stabilise after sharp dip last year
summary:
Hong Kong’s apparel imports showed early stabilisation in January–February 2026, rising 0.7 per cent YoY to $946.49 million after a steep 2025 decline. 
Casualwear led gains, with T-shirts and jerseys increasing share, while structured wear softened. 
Despite this uptick, imports remain well below 2024 levels, reflecting cautious sourcing and weak global demand.

title: FTAs, PTAs in focus as Sri Lanka aims for growth
summary:
Industry stakeholders highlight that Sri Lanka faces a competitive disadvantage in global markets due to limited preferential trade agreements, unlike many competitors, making securing free trade agreements (FTAs) and preferential trade agreements (PTAs) critical. 
Industry stakeholders call upon the government to accelerate negotiations on FTAs and PTAs.

title: Italy’s apparel export-import plunge after positive trend in 2025
summary:
Italy’s sharp decline in apparel trade in early 2026 reflects short-term demand caution and inventory tightening rather than structural weakness.
Strong 2025 performance highlights resilient trade fundamentals supported by diversified markets.
Gradual sourcing shifts, including reduced reliance on China, indicate evolving supply chain strategies.

title: US' Kontoor Brands appoints Erinn Murphy to lead finance role
summary:
Kontoor Brands has named Erinn Murphy VP, global head of finance & operations for Helly Hansen and Corporate Investor Relations, starting May in Oslo. 
She joins from Crocs, Inc., bringing nearly two decades of experience across investor relations, strategy and equity research. 
Michael Karapetian will expand his role and return in Q3 2026 to support transition and investor engagement.

title: Switzerland’s apparel imports grow double-digit in Jan–Feb 2026
summary:
Switzerland’s apparel imports rose 11.7 per cent year on year (YoY) to $1.523 billion in January–February 2026, signalling steady demand. 
Growth builds on 2025 momentum, led by knitwear and comfort-led segments. 
China, Bangladesh and Italy remain key suppliers. 
Strong purchasing power and stable retail trends continue to support consistent sourcing activity.

title: Marks & Spencer renews strategic partnership with TCS
summary:
Marks and Spencer have renewed its long-standing strategic partnership with TCS to accelerate its technology transformation.
The collaboration will focus on building an omnichannel, data-driven retail model powered by AI and modern technologies, enhancing customer experience while supporting long-term growth and operational agility. 
It will also strengthen digital capabilities across core business function

title: Trade bodies call for moving HR 4930 forward in US legislative process
summary:
AAFA along with 18 other trade bodies recently wrote to Congressional leadership in the US House of Representatives seeking support to move HR 4930 forward in the legislative process. 
The piece of legislation, aimed at addressing long-standing challenges to the enforcement of IPR at US borders, was reported with unanimous, bipartisan support from the House Ways and Means Committee.

title: Organic Cotton Summit 2026 targets supply chain, production challenges
summary: Efforts to strengthen global supply chains and scale sustainable cotton production will take centre stage at the Organic Cotton Summit in Istanbul.

title: Inspectorio AI platform to enhance Gap supply chain oversight
summary: Gap uses Inspectorio’s AI platform to increase visibility, quality management and supplier collaboration within its network.

title: How AI, resale, nearshoring are reshaping apparel in 2026
summary: After a GlobalData report outlined the trends set to redefine apparel in 2026, Just Style examines how they're already playing out.

title: Middle East conflict expected to worsen supply strain on UK retailers
summary: The latest UK retail figures from the BRC show non-food sales increased 0.9% year-on-year in March 2026, below the 12 month average of 1.1%.

title: Matalan names new sourcing boss to streamline operations
summary: Fashion and homeware retailer Matalan is strengthening its product leadership team with two key appointments.

title: Spinnova prepares to restart Woodspin demo factory production
summary: Spinnova has commenced trial runs at its Woodspin demonstration factory in Jyväskylä, Finland, as part of efforts to restart production.

title: Fashion for Good project targets textile waste recycling at scale
summary: Fashion for Good has introduced a new project to build the infrastructure for large-scale textile-to-textile (T2T) recycling across Europe.

title: Vinted revenue tops $1bn in 2025 as secondhand market soars
summary: Secondhand marketplace Vinted has announced annual results for 2025, including total revenue of $1.1bn and net profits of $62 million.

title: Why innovation stalls between pilot and production in fashion
summary: A Transformers Foundation webinar showed that fashion’s climate innovation pipeline is active, but scale is stalling as suppliers lead while brands hold back.

title: Mothercare sales dip amid Boots exit, Middle East uncertainty
summary: Mothercare reports a 22% fall in retail sales for FY26, due to the end of its Boots partnership and instability in Middle Eastern markets.

title: A New Era of Sports Broadcasting: Galaxy S26 Ultra Unlocks New Perspectives, Bringing Cameras Inside the Action
summary: Samsung Electronics, a global partner of Street League Skateboarding (SLS) today announced that Galaxy S26 Ultra was used to film live Street League Skateboarding (SLS) competition for the first time when it captured the SLS DTLA Takeover on April 4. title: [Video] Samsung Spatial Signage: How To Make Your Brand Stand Out in Crowded Spaces
summary: Three-dimensional showcases are a proven way to capture attention in high-traffic retail and public spaces, but traditional boxy installations take up valuable floor space that many brands simply don’t have. title: Samsung Retains No. 1 Position in Global Gaming Monitor Market for Seventh Year
summary: Samsung Electronics today announced it has retained its position as the world’s No. 1 gaming monitor brand for the seventh consecutive year, continuing a run that started in 2019. title: Samsung Study Highlights Rising Privacy Concerns in Public Smartphone Use
summary: A new Samsung Electronics study reveals widespread exposure of personal content during public smartphone use across Europe. Surveying 11,000 people, the research found that 56% have accidentally viewed someone else’s screen, while 24% admitted doing so out of curiosity. The findings highlight a gap between perceived and actual privacy. While 48% believe their phone use […]
